NatWest Group plc Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
NatWest Group plc reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported net interest income was GBP 3,268 million compared to GBP 2,899 million a year ago. Net income was GBP 1,680 million compared to GBP 1,245 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 0.198 compared to GBP 0.141 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 0.196 compared to GBP 0.14 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was GBP 0.198 compared to GBP 0.141 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was GBP 0.196 compared to GBP 0.14 a year ago.
For the nine months, net interest income was GBP 9,388 million compared to GBP 8,307 million a year ago. Net income was GBP 4,354 million compared to GBP 3,473 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 0.507 compared to GBP 0.382 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was GBP 0.502 compared to GBP 0.379 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was GBP 0.507 compared to GBP 0.383 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was GBP 0.502 compared to GBP 0.38 a year ago.

data.title: Hexagon AB Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Hexagon AB (publ) reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was EUR 1,303.1 million compared to EUR 1,299.8 million a year ago. Net loss was EUR 33.9 million compared to net income of EUR 235.2 million a year ago. Basic loss per share from continuing operations was EUR 0.013 compared to basic earnings per share from continuing operations of EUR 0.088 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was EUR 0.013 compared to diluted earnings per share from continuing operations of EUR 0.087 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was EUR 3,995.8 million compared to EUR 3,953.1 million a year ago. Net income was EUR 380.4 million compared to EUR 731 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was EUR 0.142 compared to EUR 0.272 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was EUR 0.141 compared to EUR 0.27 a year ago.

data.title: DIARY-Germany corporate earnings week ahead
Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC Limited reported earnings results for the second quarter and six months ended September 30, 2025. For the second quarter, the company reported sales was INR 4,613.2 million compared to INR 4,242.1 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 5,064.7 million compared to INR 5,200.5 million a year ago. Net income was INR 2,413.2 million compared to INR 2,423.4 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 8.36 compared to INR 8.41 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 8.34 compared to INR 8.39 a year ago.
For the six months, sales was INR 9,087.1 million compared to INR 8,108.3 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 10,717.4 million compared to INR 10,014.8 million a year ago. Net income was INR 5,184.3 million compared to INR 4,780.5 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 17.96 compared to INR 16.58 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 17.92 compared to INR 16.55 a year ago.

Meta Hit With EU Charges Over Handling of Illegal Content
data.description:
By Kim Mackrael


BRUSSELS--The European Union charged Meta Platforms over its systems for handling illegal content on Facebook and Instagram, the first such allegation against a social-media platform under the bloc's flagship online-content rules.

The European Commission, the EU's executive arm, on Friday said Meta fails to give users a simple way to flag illegal content. It also accused the company of not giving its users adequate tools to appeal content moderation decisions when their posts are removed or their accounts are suspended.

The charges risk provoking the ire of the Trump administration, which has complained in the past that the EU's digital regulations unfairly target U.S. companies and that the bloc is trying to censor Americans.

The preliminary charges come under EU legislation called the Digital Services Act, which aims to set standards for how tech giants including Apple, Meta and Google-parent Alphabet should police online content. Breaches of the law can lead to fines of up to 6% of a company's global annual revenue.

The EU's findings aren't final, and Meta will have a chance to review the allegations and respond to the Commission's findings.


Write to Kim Mackrael at kim.mackrael@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 0603ET

IMF Warns Asia's Growth Will Cool on Trade Risks
data.description:
The Asia-Pacific remains the fastest-growing region in the world, though higher tariffs and rising protectionism are likely to reduce demand for its exports and eventually weigh on activity, according to the International Monetary Fund. Asia Pacific Department Deputy Director Thomas Helbling spoke to Bloomberg's Stephen Engle on the sidelines of Bund Summit in Shanghai.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

VW: production secure for coming week but short-term chip supply impacts possible
data.description:
BERLIN (Reuters) -Volkswagen said on Friday that production at its German sites is secured for the coming week but short-term impacts remain possible as the carmaker works to shield its supply chains from a dispute over Dutch chipmaker Nexperia.

"Short-term impacts on the production network of the Volkswagen Group cannot generally be ruled out," a spokesperson said in a statement.

"The Volkswagen Group is currently examining alternative sourcing options in order to minimize possible effects on its supply chain," the spokesperson said, adding that the company was in close contact with potential suppliers in this regard.

(Reporting by Rachel More; editing by Matthias Williams)
